description: Measure how well a simple linear regression explains the dependent variable using R-squared, the standard error of estimate, and the overall F-test

fields.name: parameters
fields.label: k — number of estimated regression coefficients excluding intercept (default 1 for SLR)

r_squared.interpretation: Proportion of variation in Y explained by variation in X
r_squared.slr_equivalence: In SLR, R^2 equals the square of the sample correlation between X and Y

standard_error_of_estimate.interpretation: Average distance between observed Y and fitted Y_hat; in same units as Y
standard_error_of_estimate.smaller_better: A lower se implies the regression line sits closer to the observations

f_test_overall.slr_case: In SLR, k = 1, so F = SSR / (SSE / (n - 2)) with (1, n - 2) df
f_test_overall.hypothesis: H0: all slopes = 0 vs Ha: at least one slope != 0
f_test_overall.relation_to_t: In SLR, F = t^2 on slope coefficient

applications.factor_strength: R^2 of asset return on market return gauges market beta's explanatory power
applications.model_selection: Higher R^2 among nested models of same dependent variable signals better fit
applications.residual_risk: se quantifies residual volatility around the predicted Y

validation.n_above_parameters: n > k + 1 for valid degrees of freedom

compute_fit_metrics.description: Compute R^2, standard error of estimate, and F-statistic

insufficient_n.description: Sample size too small for df

errors.message: SST must be strictly positive (Y must have variation)
Need n > 2 for valid SLR degrees of freedom

anti_patterns.rule: Do not compare R^2 between models with different dependent variables
anti_patterns.why: R^2 is scale-dependent on Y; a high R^2 on log(Y) may simply reflect Y's reduced variance, not a better model

anti_patterns.rule: Do not treat a high R^2 as proof of a good model
anti_patterns.why: R^2 says nothing about unbiasedness, causality, or out-of-sample accuracy; a mis-specified model can still fit in-sample well

anti_patterns.rule: Do not report R^2 without the sample size
anti_patterns.why: Small-n R^2 is upward biased — use adjusted R^2 for multiple regression or report (R^2, n) together

anti_patterns.rule: Do not confuse a significant F with a meaningful model
anti_patterns.why: With large n, tiny R^2 can yield a significant F but carry little economic value; always inspect effect size
